title: asarina scandens
intro: Asarina scandens, also known as creeping Asarina or creeping snakewort, is a type of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Asarina scandens include reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable plant for natural remedies. Asarina scandens can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, where the leaves and stems can be dried and infused in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
